name: HYDATIDIFORM MOLE
definition: MOLAR PREGNANCY pregnancy failure ← implantation of nonviable fertilized egg molar degeneration of one conceptus of a dizygotic twin pregnancy with same risk of CHORIOADENOMA DESTRUENS locally invasive nonmetastasizing neoplasm areas of molar change alternating with normal villi + nonviable fetus with significant

demographics.Histo: generalized hydropic swelling of all chorionic villi with prominent acellular space

dominant_finding: predominantly hypointense heterogeneous mass distending uterine cavity on T1WI
distribution: ± focal areas of hemorrhage + cystic spaces
discriminating_features: predominantly hypointense heterogeneous mass distending uterine cavity on T1WI
± focal areas of hemorrhage + cystic spaces

US: hyperechoic to moderately echogenic central uterine mass interspersed with punctate
numerous discrete cystic spaces (= hydropic villi) within a central area of heterogeneous
increased vascularity with low-resistance waveforms in spiral arteries of uterus
large hyperechoic areas (= blood clot) + areas of cystic degeneration resembling
single large central fluid collection with hyperechoic rim mimicking an anembryonic
no fetal parts / no chorionic membrane
bilateral theca lutein cysts (18-37-50%) → may take 4 months to regress after
MR: predominantly hypointense heterogeneous mass distending uterine cavity on T1WI
± focal areas of hemorrhage + cystic spaces
normal gestation with placenta + separate typical echogenic material of a hydatidiform
irregular focal hyperechoic region within myometrium
bilateral theca lutein cysts, 4-8 cm in size
nearly always coexistent fetus with severe abnormalities + IUGR

clinical.Prognosis: noninvasive in 85%, locally invasive in 13%, metastasizing in 2%
